steps.title: Build two promotion totals
steps.content: The first class is free and the second class is half price, so 2 classes cost 13.20 dollars. A third class adds the regular 26.40 dollars, so 3 classes cost 39.60 dollars. Put $(2,13.20)$ and $(3,39.60)$ in Desmos and fit a line.

steps.title: Use the fitted cost line
steps.content: The regression gives $m=26.40$ and $b=-39.60$, so the slope-intercept form is $f(x)=26.40x-39.60$. Choice B, $26.40(x-2)+13.20$, expands to $26.40x-52.80+13.20=26.40x-39.60$, so it matches.

steps.title: Confirm by substitution
steps.content: For $x=2$, choice B gives $26.40(0)+13.20=13.20$. For $x=3$, it gives $26.40(1)+13.20=39.60$. Those match the table. The other choices either charge too many full-price classes or too many half-price classes. Choice B is correct.
